# Test Quality Review — `test/features/agents/ui/evolution/`

**Scope:** 23 test files (~9 633 lines) + corresponding impl files under `lib/features/agents/ui/evolution/` (~5 715 lines excluding generated files).  
Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis).

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../evolution_chat_state_test.dart` — 2 403 lines for 41 tests (58 lines/test average). The file has 7 distinct logical groups (`build`, `sendMessage`, `rejectProposal`, `GenUI surface drain`, `GenUI proposal actions`, `GenUI soul proposal actions`, `approveProposal edge cases`, `endSession`, `rejectProposal edge cases`, `approveSoulProposal`, `rejectSoulProposal`). Split into at minimum: `evolution_chat_state_build_test.dart` (~300 lines), `evolution_chat_state_send_message_test.dart` (~600 lines), `evolution_chat_state_proposals_test.dart` (~500 lines), `evolution_chat_state_genui_test.dart` (~500 lines), `evolution_chat_state_lifecycle_test.dart` (~200 lines). Shared `_stubDefaultSession()` helper would eliminate ~40 inline `startSession`/`abandonSession` stub blocks. **RESOLVED (adapted):** the multi-file split would violate the one-test-file-per-source rule (the source is a single 591-line Riverpod codegen controller whose `state`-writing methods cannot move out, so no source seam exists). The DRY half was done instead: `stubSuccessfulStart` is now used by every canonical success-start site (including custom-session GenUI variants via its `session:` parameter, with test-specific `getCurrentProposal` re-stubs relying on mocktail last-wins), and a new `stubNullStart()` helper replaced the 7 repeated null-start stub pairs. File is down to 1 901 lines with zero inline canonical stub blocks remaining.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../soul_evolution_chat_state_test.dart` — 1 789 lines for 33 tests (54 lines/test average). Logical groups (`build`, `sendMessage`, `approveSoulProposal`, `rejectSoulProposal`, `endSession`, `event handler wiring`, `dispose error path`, `surface messages`) map cleanly to 3–4 split files. The file already has an excellent `stubSuccessfulStart` helper, but it does not reduce overall file size. **RESOLVED (adapted):** same rule conflict as the item above — the 379-line codegen controller offers no source seam, so the file split is rejected; instead the remaining 7 inline success-start triples were converted to `stubSuccessfulStart(session: ...)` and the 5 repeated null-start pairs to a new `stubNullStart()` helper (file now 1 708 lines).

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../evolution_chat_page_test.dart` — 752 lines with 19 tests (40 lines/test). The pop-navigation test at line 533–596 contains a hand-built `ProviderScope`/`MaterialApp` tree that bypasses `makeTestableWidgetNoScroll`. Extract to `_buildNavigationSubject()` or consolidate with `buildSubject`. **RESOLVED:** done — `makeTestableWidgetNoScroll` gained a `navigatorKey` parameter and the pop test now routes through it with the same override set; the hand-built tree is gone.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../soul_evolution_chat_page_test.dart` — 627 lines with 25 tests. Three fake notifier classes (`_FakeSoulEvolutionChatState`, `_TrackingSoulEvolutionChatState`, `_ControllableSoulEvolutionChatState`) are structurally identical to the three in `evolution_chat_page_test.dart`. Both files define `buildSubject` boilerplate with the same shape. A shared test utilities file (e.g., `test/features/agents/ui/evolution/evolution_chat_test_utils.dart`) could eliminate ~150 lines of duplication. **RESOLVED:** done — `evolution_chat_test_utils.dart` hosts all six fakes (the template and soul controllers are distinct riverpod codegen classes, so each keeps a thin subclass, defined once); both page tests import it.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/.../evolution_dashboard_header.dart` — 394 lines for a single widget; consider splitting collapsed/expanded state into a sub-widget to make testing easier.
  **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) this is a source-architecture suggestion, not a test-quality gap. The stated motivation ("to make testing easier") is already moot — the dashboard header is fully exercised, including the compact-view numeric assertions ('7' wakes, compact-formatted 1234 tokens) and the dedicated expanded-view test pinning the decimal-formatted lifetimeWakeCount 42 (see the now-resolved coverage item below). Splitting a 394-line widget that has comprehensive coverage purely on a speculative testability ground would be a risky source refactor outside the test-quality mandate with no measurable test benefit.

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../evolution_chat_state_test.dart` lines 62–2089 — No shared `_stubDefaultSession()` helper. The three-statement triple `when(startSession) / when(getActiveSessionForTemplate) / when(abandonSession)` block appears **39 times** with near-identical content. Extract to a file-level helper (or reuse the `stubSuccessfulStart` pattern from `soul_evolution_chat_state_test.dart`) to eliminate ~500 lines of copy-paste stub code. Per AGENTS.md "Mock setup must not dwarf test logic." **RESOLVED:** `stubSuccessfulStart({response, session})` + `makeSession()` added; 22 canonical stub triples collapsed (file 2403 → 2056 lines); remaining sites are variant scenarios (null responses, custom proposals).

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../evolution_recorder_test_utils.dart` line 7 — `class MockRealtimeService extends Mock implements RealtimeTranscriptionService {}` is an inline mock when `MockRealtimeTranscriptionService` already exists at `test/mocks/mocks.dart:507`. Inline mock should be deleted and the import updated to the centralized version.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../soul_evolution_chat_state_test.dart` lines 1298–1314, 1455–1462, 1529–1536, 1603–1610 — The async-chain drain pattern `for (var i = 0; i < 10; i++) { await Future<void>.microtask(() {}); }` appears four times. Extract to a named helper (e.g., `_pumpMicrotasks(10)`) or replace with `await Future<void>.value()` where a single microtask cycle suffices, to improve legibility and reduce DRY violations. **RESOLVED:** (stale) the four drain loops no longer exist — the file was rewritten onto deterministic awaits during the HIGH pass.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../soul_evolution_chat_page_test.dart` lines 24–68 — Three local fake-notifier classes (`_FakeSoulEvolutionChatState`, `_TrackingSoulEvolutionChatState`, `_ControllableSoulEvolutionChatState`) mirror counterparts in `evolution_chat_page_test.dart` with only the base class name changed. No shared base or utility; each file defines its own `buildSubject` with equivalent `makeTestableWidgetNoScroll` + provider override structure. Consolidate into `evolution_chat_test_utils.dart`. **RESOLVED:** done — see above; the three soul fakes now live in the shared utils file as `FakeSoulEvolutionChatState` / `TrackingSoulEvolutionChatState` / `ControllableSoulEvolutionChatState`.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../evolution_chat_page_test.dart` lines 533–596 — Pop-navigation test builds its own `ProviderScope`/`MaterialApp`/`localizationsDelegates` tree rather than using `makeTestableWidgetNoScroll`. Per AGENTS.md rules this violates "Use `makeTestableWidget()` for widget tests." Refactor using a `_buildNavigationSubject(navigatorKey)` variant of the existing `buildSubject`. **RESOLVED:** done — same fix as the item above: the test pumps `makeTestableWidgetNoScroll(..., navigatorKey: navigatorKey)`.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/.../evolution_circle_button_test.dart` line 27–32 — Test "renders the given icon" asserts only `findsOneWidget` for the icon. This is a structural-existence check only. Add a meaningful companion assertion: e.g., that `IconButton.onPressed` is null (disabled state), or that tapping produces no callback.
  **RESOLVED:** done — the test now also asserts `IconButton.onPressed` is null (disabled in the default no-`onPressed`/`forceActive: false` case) and that the rendered icon color is the inactive/muted color (`isNot(Colors.white)`), distinguishing it from the active state.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/.../evolution_transcription_progress_test.dart` lines 17–23 — Test "shows partial transcript text" asserts text presence and icon presence in separate tests but both tests pump the same widget with the same data. Consolidate into one test that asserts both, and add a test for empty/blank partial transcript to confirm graceful rendering.
  **RESOLVED:** done — the two single-pump tests are merged into one that asserts the transcript text, the `Icons.transcribe` icon, and the `CircularProgressIndicator` together; a new test pumps an empty partial transcript and asserts the descendant `Text.data == ''` plus the icon/progress affordances still render (no overflow/crash).

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../evolution_chat_state.dart` lines 352–359 — `_isImplicitApprovalMessage(String text)` is a pure static function that applies a Unicode-aware regex to trim-and-match. This is exactly the profile for Glados: test the property that every variant in the regex pattern (`ok`, `okay`, `yes`, `yep`, `approve`, `approved`, `lgtm`, `sounds good`, `looks good`, `ship it`) returns `true` regardless of surrounding punctuation/whitespace, and that arbitrary strings not matching those anchors return `false`. Currently only `'ok'` and `'yes'` are exercised in the test suite, leaving `'okay'`, `'yep'`, `'lgtm'`, `'sounds good'`, `'looks good'`, `'approve'`, `'approved'`, and `'ship it'` uncovered, as well as the punctuation/whitespace boundary conditions the regex explicitly handles. **RESOLVED:** done — `debugIsImplicitApprovalMessage` seam added; a Glados3 property runs all ten keywords × punctuation/whitespace decorations (incl. uppercase), and a companion property proves the anchor rejects keywords embedded in longer sentences.

  ```dart
  // Candidate property:
  Glados<String>(any.string).test(
    'all canonical approval words match regardless of surrounding punctuation',
    (padding) { ... },
    tags: 'glados',
  );
  ```

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../evolution_chat_state.dart` lines 364–370 — `_proposalKey(PendingProposal)` is a pure function (join of three trimmed strings). A Glados round-trip property (`decode(encode(p)) == encode(p)` for idempotence) would confirm trimming is stable and separator is not present in proposal text. **RESOLVED:** done — `debugProposalKey` seam added; the property asserts trim-stability (padding any field leaves the fingerprint unchanged) and the exact three-field separator structure.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/.../evolution_chat_state.dart` line 361 — `_hasNonEmptyText(String?)` is a one-liner, but a Glados property ("only whitespace-only and null inputs return false; all others return true") documents the contract explicitly and catches future whitespace handling changes.
  **RESOLVED:** done — added a `debugHasNonEmptyText` `@visibleForTesting` seam (matching the existing two seams) and a `debugHasNonEmptyText — properties` group: an explicit null case, a Glados property over whitespace-only paddings (must be `false`), and a Glados3 property that wraps a `glados.any.letterOrDigits` payload (forced non-empty) in whitespace on both sides and asserts `true`, documenting the "any non-whitespace char ⇒ non-empty" contract.

No Glados candidates exist in the UI/widget tests in this directory — correctly, per the UI-test exclusion in `test/README.md`.

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/.../widgets/evolution_cancel_stop_buttons.dart` (41 lines) — No test file. Covers cancel/stop button rendering and callback wiring; straightforward to test with callback capture pattern already used in voice-controls tests. **RESOLVED:** new evolution_cancel_stop_buttons_test (icons, tooltips, per-button callback isolation).

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/.../widgets/ritual_summary_card.dart` (128 lines) — No test file. Widget is used by the review pages; missing tests for summary rendering, empty-state, and wakeCount display. **RESOLVED:** new ritual_summary_card_test (title/subtitle, number-formatted metric tiles, chart wiring, empty-history collapse, zero metrics).

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/.../widgets/evolution_wake_activity_chart.dart` (155 lines) — No test file. Chart rendering for varying data-point counts (0, 1, many) is the same pattern already tested in sparkline and wake-bar tests; straightforward to add. **RESOLVED:** new evolution_wake_activity_chart_test (empty → zero-size, single bucket, height-factor scaling against the busiest day, quartile date labels for 30 days).

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../evolution_chat_state.dart` line 352–359 — `_isImplicitApprovalMessage` tested only for `'ok'` (line 544) and `'yes'` (line 710). Seven other approval keywords (`'okay'`, `'yep'`, `'approve'`, `'approved'`, `'lgtm'`, `'sounds good'`, `'looks good'`, `'ship it'`) and the punctuation/whitespace boundary conditions (e.g., `'  ok!  '`, `'(ok)'`) are never exercised. Also: negative case — a long message that contains "ok" but should NOT match (e.g., `'this is not ok behavior'`) has no coverage to confirm the anchored regex rejects it. **RESOLVED:** covered by the same property pair — every keyword is asserted to match standalone (with decorations) and to NOT match inside 'this is not <kw> behavior' / '<kw> but please tweak…'.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../evolution_chat_state.dart` lines 507–541 (`approveSoulProposal`) — The `soul_proposal_failed` branch (null version return, line 525) has a test, but the exception path (catch block line 533) has test coverage only via a `// Should not throw` assertion without verifying state is preserved (`data` is `isNotNull`). The state-preservation invariant after the catch is not asserted. **RESOLVED:** done — the catch-path test now additionally asserts the session id survives ('session-1') and the message list is intact alongside `isWaiting == false`.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../widgets/evolution_transcript_container.dart` (43 lines) — No test file. **RESOLVED:** added — asserts the themed surface chrome (fill/radius/outline/shadow), token-driven padding, and the 120px height cap with an oversized child.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../widgets/feedback_helpers.dart` (18 lines) — No test file. If this contains pure helper functions they are trivially testable. **RESOLVED:** added — exhaustively maps every `FeedbackCategory` against the live ARB bundle and asserts the labels are non-empty and pairwise distinct.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../widgets/evolution_dashboard_header.dart` (394 lines) — Only collapse/expand is tested at a high level; the metric display values (`lifetimeWakeCount`, `wakesSinceLastSession`, token count) are asserted as labels but actual numeric values rendered from `RitualSummaryMetrics` are not verified (e.g., asserting `find.text('42')` for `lifetimeWakeCount: 42`). **RESOLVED:** done — the compact view now asserts '7' wakes and the compact-formatted 1234 tokens; a new expanded-view test pins the decimal-formatted lifetimeWakeCount 42.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/.../evolution_chat_state_test.dart` line 2092–2104 (`EvolutionChatData.copyWith` test) — Only tests `isWaiting` field; `copyWith` has six nullable-function parameters (`sessionId`, `processor`, `lastSurfacedProposalKey`) that use the `Function()?` erasure pattern. The null-erasing behaviour (passing a `() => null` lambda) is not tested, so a regression in the nullable copyWith pattern would go undetected.
  **RESOLVED:** done — the `EvolutionChatData` group gained three tests covering the full `Function()?` erasure contract for all three nullable fields (`sessionId`, `processor` via centralized `MockSurfaceController`, `lastSurfacedProposalKey`): (1) omitting the function params preserves the existing values, (2) passing `() => null` erases each to null, (3) passing a value-returning function sets each to the new value. A fourth test verifies `messages`/`categoryRatings` replacement leaves the source instance untouched.

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../evolution_chat_state_test.dart` — Each of 41 tests creates its own `ProviderContainer`, resolves the full async provider chain, and performs 2–7 stub interactions. The container factory (`createContainer()`) is well-extracted, but **there is no `setUp`/`setUpAll` that shares mock stub defaults across tests**. The 40 near-identical `when(mockWorkflow.startSession)` + `when(mockWorkflow.abandonSession)` calls (~80 total `when` stubs) execute per-test. Extracting a `_stubDefaultSession()` helper (as `soul_evolution_chat_state_test.dart` does with `stubSuccessfulStart`) would reduce setup overhead and improve readability, with a modest CI speed benefit across 40 tests. **RESOLVED:** see line 59 — the shared stub helper now covers the canonical sessions.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../soul_evolution_chat_state_test.dart` lines 1296–1313, 1453–1461, 1527–1535, 1601–1609 — Four separate event-handler tests each spin `10` `Future.microtask` iterations in a `for` loop to drain the async chain. This is fragile busy-waiting that relies on a loop count that may need to grow if more async hops are added. Replace with `await Future<void>.value()` twice (one await per async `await` level in the chain), or restructure the test to await the future returned by the wired callback's target method directly, removing the loop entirely. Impact: smaller test bodies, no silent fragility under deeper async chains.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../soul_evolution_chat_state_test.dart` line 1657 — `await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` inside the "dispose abandon error path" test. This is an explicit fake-time policy violation (`test/README.md` §Fake Time Policy Rule 3). Replace with `await Future<void>.value()` (microtask flush) since the test is not inside `fakeAsync` and the unawaited dispose future only requires a microtask boundary to settle. **RESOLVED:** done — replaced with `pumpEventQueue()` (bounded event-queue drain, no zero-duration Timer).

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../evolution_chat_page_test.dart` — 15 of 19 widget tests use `pumpAndSettle()`. Several tests (e.g., lines 148–159 loading indicator, lines 424–435 hide-during-loading) confirm that providers are still pending, meaning nothing settles. These tests correctly use `pump()` + `pump()` instead, but the majority of the other tests use `pumpAndSettle()` for simple Riverpod `AsyncValue` resolution. Since these use faked providers that resolve synchronously, a single `pump()` followed by `pump()` is deterministic and faster than running `pumpAndSettle()`'s default 100-frame loop. Estimated impact: ~12 tests × the difference between 1 extra frame vs. up to 100 frames. **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) tried empirically — the stacked async overrides (template + metrics + chat state, and the error-future variants) need a VARIABLE number of frames, and bounded two/four-pump variants produced deterministic failures; with no continuous animations `pumpAndSettle` here terminates in 2-3 frames, so it is the correct tool.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../soul_evolution_chat_page_test.dart` — Same pattern: 25 `testWidgets` calls, the majority using `pumpAndSettle()` for synchronously-resolving overridden providers. Prefer `pump(); pump();` for loading-then-settled sequences. **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) same empirical result as the sibling item — bounded pumps failed for the multi-layer provider resolution; the settles terminate quickly.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/.../evolution_session_timeline_test.dart` (404 lines, 10+ tests) — Each test rebuilds the full `EvolutionSessionTimeline` + `ProviderContainer` independently. The timeline widget has no inter-test state; a `setUpAll` with `buildSubject` and a single `pumpWidget` shared between read-only tests (e.g., empty-state, loading indicator) could reduce pumping overhead.
  **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) the proposed optimization is infeasible under `flutter_test`. Each `testWidgets` block receives its own `WidgetTester` bound to a fresh per-test `TestWidgetsFlutterBinding`; a widget tree pumped inside one `testWidgets` callback (or in `setUpAll`, which runs outside any `WidgetTester`) cannot be carried into a different `testWidgets` callback. Every assertion here also drives a *different* provider override (empty list, pending `Completer`, error future, varied session lists), so even the data differs per test — there is no genuinely shared read-only pump to hoist. The current per-test `buildSubject` + `pumpAndSettle` is the only correct structure.

---

## Summary

- **2 oversized impl files** above 500 lines (`evolution_chat_state.dart` at 591, `evolution_review_page.dart` at 432 — borderline).
- **2 critically oversized test files** (`evolution_chat_state_test.dart` at 2 403 lines, `soul_evolution_chat_state_test.dart` at 1 789 lines) — both are prime split candidates.
- **5 weak test / quality issues**: inline duplicate mock (`MockRealtimeService`), missing `_stubDefaultSession` helper in largest test file, ad-hoc `ProviderScope` tree in pop-navigation test, repeated microtask-drain loop pattern (×4), and uncovered `copyWith` nullable-erasing branches.
- **0 Glados tests** currently exist for this package; 3 genuine pure-logic candidates identified (`_isImplicitApprovalMessage`, `_proposalKey`, `_hasNonEmptyText`), with `_isImplicitApprovalMessage` being the highest-value target.
- **5 missing test files** (`evolution_cancel_stop_buttons.dart`, `evolution_transcript_container.dart`, `evolution_wake_activity_chart.dart`, `feedback_helpers.dart`, `ritual_summary_card.dart`).
- **4 speed wins** identified: remove `Future.delayed(Duration.zero)` fake-time violation, replace 4× microtask spin loops with deterministic awaits, prefer `pump()` over `pumpAndSettle()` for synchronous provider fakes, and extract shared stub helpers to remove 80+ redundant mock-setup calls.
- **Biggest single opportunity**: Split `evolution_chat_state_test.dart` and extract a `_stubDefaultSession()` helper — this single change would eliminate ~500 lines of copy-paste stub code, reduce the file from 2 403 to ~800–900 lines across 3–4 focused files, and make failures far easier to triage.
